title: Wire DeckSlotPolicy.Validate into deck generation and archetype ranking
description:
After DeckSlotPolicy and SlotType metadata exist (child task 1), integrate validation into the recommendation pipeline so policy-violating decks are never surfaced to the user.

## Integration points

### recommend.GenerateDeck
- After assembling a candidate deck, call DeckSlotPolicy.Validate(deck)
- If validation fails, skip this candidate (don't add to results)
- Policy should be instantiated once per call (1 evo + 1 champ + 1 flex = 3 total)

### Archetype ranking
- internal/recommend/archetype.go or equivalent
- When scoring archetypes, down-rank or discard any archetype whose canonical deck violates the slot policy
- Don't silently swap cards — surface the violation in the archetype's notes/warnings

## What NOT to do
- Do not silently drop evos or champions from a deck to make it valid
- Do not modify the policy constants — they come from slot_policy.go (child task 1)
- Do not break existing tests; update test fixtures to use valid slot assignments

## Files to edit
- internal/recommend/recommend.go (or wherever GenerateDeck lives)
- internal/recommend/archetype.go (or equivalent ranking logic)
- Relevant test files
acceptance_criteria: Policy-violating decks (2+ champions, Balloon evo in regular evo slot) are never returned by GenerateDeck; archetype ranking down-ranks or discards slot-policy-violating archetypes; violations surfaced in notes/warnings (not silently dropped); existing tests pass with slot-valid fixtures
close_reason: Implemented in pkg/recommend/slot_policy.go, slot_metadata.go, slot_combinator.go; wired into recommender.go

title: Add SlotType metadata to cards: RegularEvo, ChampionSlotOnlyEvo, Champion
description:
Create per-card SlotType metadata and a DeckSlotPolicy struct that validates a deck against game rules.

## Card classification (three types)
- **RegularEvo**: standard evolved card (e.g., Skeleton Army evo, Witch evo, Goblin Barrel evo)
  - Can occupy: evo slot OR flex slot
- **ChampionSlotOnlyEvo**: evolved card that only occupies the champion or flex slot (e.g., Balloon evo)
  - Can occupy: champion slot OR flex slot — NEVER the regular evo slot
- **Champion**: dedicated champion card (e.g., Miner, Archer Queen, Bowler-as-champion)
  - Can occupy: champion slot OR flex slot

## Important distinction being captured (reason this task exists)
Bowler has both a regular version (spell/tank) and appears as a champion. Must NOT conflate the two.
Balloon evo is NOT a regular evo — it occupies champion/flex slot only.

## DeckSlotPolicy struct to define
```go
type SlotType int
const (
    RegularCard SlotType = iota
    RegularEvo
    ChampionSlotOnlyEvo
    Champion
)

type DeckSlotPolicy struct {
    MaxEvoSlots     int // = 1 (regular evo slot)
    MaxChampionSlots int // = 1 (champion slot)
    MaxFlexSlots    int // = 1 (flex: evo or champ)
}

func (p DeckSlotPolicy) Validate(deck []CardWithSlotType) error
```

## Files to create/edit
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_policy.go (policy struct + Validate)
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_metadata.go (per-card SlotType table, initially hand-curated)
- Tests: internal/recommend/slot_policy_test.go
acceptance_criteria: SlotType enum with 4 values (RegularCard, RegularEvo, ChampionSlotOnlyEvo, Champion) defined in slot_policy.go; DeckSlotPolicy.Validate() returns error for slot-rule violations; Balloon evo classified as ChampionSlotOnlyEvo; Bowler champion distinguishable from regular Bowler; unit tests in slot_policy_test.go cover all slot types and Validate()
close_reason: Implemented in pkg/recommend/slot_policy.go, slot_metadata.go, slot_combinator.go; wired into recommender.go

title: Model deck evo/champion slot policy and best-assignment combinator
description:
Clash Royale decks have exactly 3 special card slots, but the current codebase has ZERO modeling of this constraint.

## Slot Rules (game mechanics)

- **Evo slot** (1 max): regular evolved cards only — NOT for champion-slot-only evos
- **Champion slot** (1 max): champion cards OR champion-slot-only evos (e.g., Balloon evo)
- **Flex slot** (1 max): either a regular evo or a champion
- **Total special cards**: max 3 (some combination of evos + champions, subject to the above)

Examples of valid assignments:
  - Balloon-evo (champion slot) + Skel-Army-evo (evo slot) + Witch-evo (flex slot) → 2 evos + 0 champs + Balloon in champ slot
  - Balloon-evo (champion slot) + Miner (flex as champion) + Skel-Army-evo (evo slot) → 2 evos + 1 champ

## Known card-type metadata (must be captured)

- Balloon: champion-slot-only evo — cannot go in the regular evo slot
- All standard champions (Miner, Archer Queen, etc.): champion slot or flex slot

## What needs to be built

1. **DeckSlotPolicy struct** with Validate(deck) — model the constraint at deck level; include per-card SlotType metadata (RegularEvo / ChampionSlotOnlyEvo / Champion)
2. **Filter integration** — wire Validate() into recommend.GenerateDeck and archetype ranking so policy-violating decks are rejected
3. **Assignment combinator** — given candidate evos and champions in a deck, enumerate all valid (EvoSlot, FlexSlot, ChampionSlot) assignments, score each via the evaluator, return the top-ranked assignment for display

## Success Criteria
- SlotType metadata implemented for all current CR cards (task `she`)
- DeckSlotPolicy.Validate() wired into recommend.GenerateDeck and archetype ranking (task `77j`)
- Slot assignment combinator enumerates and scores all valid (evo, evo, champion) assignments (task `sl2`)
- No generated deck violates the 3-slot constraint (1 evo + 1 champ + 1 flex)
- Balloon evo and standard champions always placed in game-legal slots in all outputs
notes:

title: Fix deck builder: enforce 1-champion limit and model evolution/champion slot tradeoffs
description:
Two related correctness issues in the deck builder around CR's special slot system:

## Bug: No 1-champion-per-deck enforcement
The builder currently emits decks with 2-3 Champions (e.g. Golden Knight + Skeleton King + Mighty Miner in the same AGGRO deck). Clash Royale only allows 1 Champion per deck. There is zero enforcement anywhere in the builder or generator.

Fix: add a championCount constraint to pkg/deck/builder.go fillRemainingSlots / role selection — once 1 Champion rarity card is in the deck, exclude all other Champion-rarity cards from consideration.

## Feature gap: Champion vs Evolution slot tradeoff not modeled
CR's slot system has distinct constraints for Champions and Evolutions:
- 1 Champion slot (max 1 champion per deck)
- Up to 2 Evolution slots (evolvable cards, scored via EvolutionScorer)
- A flex slot that can hold either an additional Evolution OR counts toward the champion slot

The builder currently treats these as fully independent: evolutionSlotLimit=2 and champion is just a rarity multiplier. The strategic tradeoff of 'should this flex slot be a Champion (ability value) or an evolved card (evolution bonus)?' is never evaluated.

Fix: model the slot system explicitly — track filled champion slots and evolution slots separately during deck construction, evaluate both options for flex slot, and score the tradeoff using the champion ability metadata (see clash-royale-api-ite) vs evolution bonus.

Key files:
- pkg/deck/builder.go: fillRemainingSlots(), selectEvolutionSlots(), scoreCard()
- pkg/deck/types.go: CardCandidate struct may need IsChampion bool
- internal/config/rarity.go: champion card list already exists here

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `cr-api deck recommend --tag <player-tag>` for any player
2. Inspect the generated decks in the output
3. Observe that multiple Champion-rarity cards (Golden Knight, Skeleton King, Mighty Miner, etc.) can appear in the same deck
4. Confirm against Clash Royale rules: only 1 Champion is allowed per deck
acceptance_criteria: No generated deck contains more than 1 Champion-rarity card; evolution vs champion slot tradeoff is visible in --verbose scoring output; existing suite tests pass
close_reason: Added championLimit (default 1) to Builder; enforced in fillRemainingSlots and post-build enforcement; excess champions replaced with non-champion candidates; RarityChampion constant extracted

title: Add 'cr-api review --tag' orchestrator command
description: A full player review currently requires running 11+ separate sub-commands (player, playstyle, analyze, upgrade-impact, archetypes detect, deck recommend, deck build, deck evaluate, evolutions recommend, deck mulligan, what-if). Add a top-level 'cr-api review --tag <tag>' that bundles: profile snapshot + playstyle summary + top archetype from 'archetypes detect' + top 3 upgrade priorities (cross-archetype impact) + current-deck-vs-best-recommended-deck delta (score, level fit, archetype) + budget-aware 'next 20k gold' suggestion. Output should support --format=human|json|markdown. Lands in the same cr-api binary, not a separate sub-binary. Reuses existing analyzers; this is an orchestrator, not new analysis logic.
close_reason: review orchestrator fully shipped; all children (C1-C4) resolved

title: Fix deck recommend synergy column always reporting 0.0%
description: Found while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV. 'cr-api deck recommend --tag R8QGUQRCV --count 3' reports 'Synergy: 0.0%' for every deck (Deck #1, #2, and #3 all show 0.0%) while 'Compatibility' and 'Overall' have real values. title: Unify genetic search config across discover and fuzz
description:
Triage finding 2026-07-12 duplication/refactor pass. Genetic-search configuration is currently expressed in three separate shapes that drift independently: `cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go` defines `generations`, `population`, `mutation-rate`, `crossover-rate`, island-model flags plus manual background-process argument forwarding; `cmd/cr-api/deck_fuzz_commands.go` exposes a second GA flag stack under `ga-*`; `cmd/cr-api/fuzz_commands.go` separately maps those flags back into `genetic.GeneticConfig`. Nearby, `pkg/deck/generator_iterators.go` maintains another GA-shaped config/result surface already tracked by a separate issue, which makes the command-layer duplication even easier to drift.

Refactor toward one canonical CLI-to-genetic-config adapter for shared GA knobs and defaults. `discover` and `fuzz` can still keep command-specific flags where behavior truly differs, but shared population/generation/mutation/crossover/island settings should come from one helper or config layer instead of being re-declared and re-forwarded by hand. Goal is to shrink command plumbing, keep defaults/messages consistent, and make future genetic tuning happen in one place.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared helper or config adapter owns the common GA flag definitions/defaults used by both `discover` and `fuzz`.
- `discover` background-resume argument forwarding no longer hardcodes the shared GA flag list separately from flag declaration.
- `fuzz_commands.go` no longer manually reassembles a parallel `genetic.GeneticConfig` from a separately named flag set without going through the shared adapter.
- Tests cover at least one shared-defaults/parsing path so future GA tuning changes fail in one place instead of silently drifting.

title: Extract shared evaluation context and leaderboard persistence pipeline
description:
Triage finding from 2026-07-09 duplication/refactor pass.

Evaluation-oriented commands repeatedly rebuild the same plumbing for player context and leaderboard persistence: evaluation.NewPlayerContextFromPlayer plus optional API fetch; leaderboard.NewStorage lifecycle; mapping evaluation results into leaderboard.DeckEntry; InsertDeck/RecalculateStats calls; and verbose warning/reporting. This logic is split across cmd/cr-api/deck_evaluate_runtime.go (persistEvaluationResult), cmd/cr-api/deck_eval_batch_runtime.go, cmd/cr-api/deck_analyze_suite_runtime.go, cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go, and cmd/cr-api/fuzz_commands.go. The implementations are close but not identical, which risks drift in storage tags, evaluation versioning, warning behavior, and stats refresh policy.

Extract a shared helper/service for optional player-context loading and evaluation result persistence, then have single-deck, batch, suite, discover, and fuzz flows wrap it for command-specific output only. Goal is to shrink command runtimes and keep storage behavior consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared helpers cover optional player-context construction and leaderboard storage lifecycle for evaluation commands.
- deck_evaluate and deck_eval_batch no longer maintain separate DeckEntry mapping/stat-refresh implementations; suite/discover/fuzz reuse the same path where applicable.
- Tests cover the shared persistence behavior and preserve current user-visible warnings or intentionally normalize them.

title: Centralize tagged artifact and report persistence helpers
description:
Triage finding from 2026-07-09 duplication/refactor pass.

cmd/cr-api/artifact_save_helpers.go already centralizes tagged JSON artifact naming plus storage.WriteJSON, but several commands still bypass it with ad hoc timestamp formatting, filename construction, directory creation, and os.WriteFile/json.MarshalIndent logic. The same persistence shape now appears in cmd/cr-api/deck_eval_batch_runtime.go (buildEvalOutputFilename + writeEvalBatchOutput), cmd/cr-api/deck_analyze_suite_runtime.go (evaluation JSON + markdown report saves), cmd/cr-api/deck_budget_runtime.go, cmd/cr-api/deck_possible_count_runtime.go, cmd/cr-api/archetype_commands.go, and cmd/cr-api/compare_commands.go.

Expand the helper layer so commands can ask for tagged/timestamped JSON, markdown, and text outputs with one naming/path policy, then migrate the remaining runtimes to it. Goal is to delete repeated path/timestamp/write code, stop output-name drift across commands, and make future artifact layout changes happen in one place.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A shared helper owns tagged/timestamped filename generation plus directory creation for JSON and non-JSON artifacts.
- deck_eval_batch, deck_analyze_suite, compare, and at least the remaining tagged JSON writers stop open-coding timestamp/path/write logic.
- Existing command output filenames remain intentional and covered by tests where behavior matters.
notes: 2026-07-10: Landed first slice of artifact persistence dedup. Added tagged text artifact helper in cmd/cr-api/artifact_save_helpers.go, routed deck evaluate-batch aggregated output through it, and moved compare report-output onto writeTextOutput so parent dirs are created consistently. Added focused tests for tagged text saves and nested output paths. Full issue remains open for suite/report/json callers still open-coding persistence.

title: Extract shared deck-hash migration runner for SQLite storage
description:
Triage finding 2026-07-05 duplication/refactor pass. pkg/leaderboard/storage.go and pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go still duplicate the same deck-hash migration orchestration even after internal/storageutil extracted row parsing and canonical winner selection. Both storages repeat: migration-applied gate, run migration, begin transaction, rollback-on-error, commit, then record migration marker; the remaining per-package variation is just table/query labels, optional invalid-JSON warning policy, and leaderboard-only stats recomputation. Extract a shared storageutil helper that runs the migration transaction and recording flow, and another small row-loading helper if it keeps the call sites short. Goal is to shrink both storage packages and keep any future migration-failure behavior consistent across leaderboard and fuzz storage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- internal/storageutil owns the shared deck-hash migration execution flow used by both pkg/leaderboard/storage.go and pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go.
- Both storage packages have less duplicated migration code and preserve current package-specific behavior, including leaderboard stats recomputation and leaderboard invalid-JSON warning handling.
- Relevant tests pass for pkg/leaderboard, pkg/fuzzstorage, and a repo-wide go test run.
close_reason: Shared deck-hash migration runner extracted into internal/storageutil and adopted by leaderboard and fuzzstorage

title: Extract shared deck input parsing/loading helpers
description: Triage finding 2026-06-27 duplication/refactor pass. Several command runtimes still reimplement the same deck input normalization path: parse deck strings, enforce exactly 8 cards, convert to deck.CardCandidate slices, and sometimes re-read analysis JSON or deck_detail payloads. Current repeated sites include cmd/cr-api/deck_evaluate_runtime.go (parseDeckString, loadDeckCardsFromInput, loadDeckFromAnalysis, loadDeckCandidatesFromFile, convertToCardCandidates), cmd/cr-api/deck_optimize_runtime.go (parseDeckString + 8-card validation in analyze/optimize flows), cmd/cr-api/compare_commands.go (per-deck parse + validation + candidate conversion), and cmd/cr-api/deck_eval_batch_runtime.go / deck_analyze_suite_runtime.go reusing only parts of the same helper surface. title: Decompose event command monolith into focused modules
description: Triage finding from 2026-06-26 duplication/refactor pass. cmd/cr-api/event_commands.go is still a ~900-line mixed-responsibility file that bundles CLI command registration, battle/event filtering helpers, collection loading, placeholder query/sort logic, tabular/markdown-style presentation, and ad hoc CSV export. Existing issue clash-royale-api-luy covers whether the event query surface should be wired or retired, but even independent of that decision the file stays harder to simplify because runtime, formatting, and persistence concerns are interleaved in one place. Split this into focused layers such as event_commands.go (registration), event_runtime.go (scan/list/analyze/compare/deck-stats actions), event_filters.go or event_query.go (shared filtering/sorting), and event_format.go / event_export.go (display + CSV/report emission). Goal is to shrink the command file, make the placeholder paths easier to replace or delete, and reduce drift between event subcommands.

title: Centralize upgrade progression lookups
description: The repo already has internal/config/upgrade.go as the claimed source of truth for upgrade progression, but production code still duplicates the same tables and lookup logic elsewhere. Current copies: internal/config/upgrade.go:9-109 owns upgradeCosts and goldCosts; pkg/budget/finder.go:477-514 redefines upgradeCosts inside calculateCardsNeededForLevel; pkg/analysis/upgrade_impact.go:718-750 redefines goldCosts inside getGoldForSingleUpgrade. This creates drift risk whenever Clash Royale changes progression again and it keeps dead-simple lookup logic spread across three packages. Refactor by exposing the minimal shared accessors from internal/config (or an import-safe package if internal boundaries block callers), then delete the duplicated maps and fallback logic from budget finder and upgrade impact analysis. Add/adjust focused tests to prove both call sites still return the same values for representative rarities and levels.
close_reason: Centralized upgrade and gold progression lookups through internal/config

title: Unify command output persistence helpers
description: Triage finding 2026-06-20 duplication/refactor pass. Several command runtimes independently implement the same output persistence pattern: choose stdout vs file, optionally create output dir, generate timestamped filenames, marshal JSON, and write reports/artifacts with slightly different UX and error handling. Current repeated sites include cmd/cr-api/compare_commands.go (writeComparisonOutput plus report-output save), cmd/cr-api/deck_eval_batch_runtime.go (writeEvalBatchOutput timestamped aggregate save), cmd/cr-api/whatif_commands.go (saveWhatIfScenario), cmd/cr-api/deck_analyze_suite_runtime.go (save evaluation JSON plus final report), and cmd/cr-api/deck_possible_count_runtime.go (direct output-file write). This is adjacent to but broader than existing artifact-save issue clash-royale-api-0co: that one covers JSON artifact save helpers in main/archetype/budget paths, while this issue targets the command-layer output contract and text/report persistence flows. Refactor toward one shared helper layer for write-to-stdout-or-file, ensure-parent-dir, timestamped output naming, and consistent user-facing save messages/errors so command files shrink and output behavior stops drifting.

Player-tag normalization is split across multiple thin wrappers and incompatible helper shapes: internal/playertag/sanitize.go owns the real validation/canonicalization, pkg/deck/player_tag.go re-exports it, internal/storage/paths.go re-exports it again, cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go wraps deck.SanitizePlayerTag in sanitizeDiscoverTag, and pkg/clashroyale/types.go has a separate NormalizeTag that only prepends '#'. There is also a nearby filename sanitizer in cmd/cr-api/deck_recommend_mulligan_runtime.go:420-440 that overlaps the same path-safety concern. Tests are duplicated too: internal/storage/paths_test.go and pkg/deck/player_tag_test.go both retest the same sanitize behavior. Refactor toward one canonical tag package/API that exposes sanitized and display forms, keep only the package-level adapters that are actually required for public surface compatibility, and delete duplicate tests/wrappers. Files to inspect first: internal/playertag/sanitize.go, pkg/deck/player_tag.go, internal/storage/paths.go, pkg/clashroyale/types.go, cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go, cmd/cr-api/deck_recommend_mulligan_runtime.go, internal/storage/paths_test.go, pkg/deck/player_tag_test.go.

The repo already has a real optimizer in pkg/deck/genetic/optimizer.go and cmd/cr-api/fuzz_commands.go uses it directly, so the current iterator duplicates naming/config/result surface without the real implementation. Refactor by either wiring generator_iterators.go to pkg/deck/genetic for actual optimization, or remove/rename the iterator and collapse its extra config/result types so the API no longer promises behavior it does not provide.

title: Combinator: enumerate and score all valid (evo, evo, champion) slot assignments
description:
Given a candidate deck that contains K evo-eligible cards and J champion-eligible cards, enumerate every valid slot assignment and score each to surface the best combination for display.

## Why this matters
For ZyLogan's deck: Balloon-evo (champion-slot-only), Skeleton Army-evo, Witch-evo, and potentially Miner (champion) are all candidates.
The combinator must output which specific assignment (e.g., 'Skel-Army in evo slot, Balloon in champion slot, Witch in flex slot') yields the highest evaluation score.

## Algorithm
1. Collect eligible cards from deck:
   - RegularEvo candidates
   - ChampionSlotOnlyEvo candidates  
   - Champion candidates
2. Enumerate all valid assignments respecting DeckSlotPolicy rules (child task 1)
3. For each assignment, call the evaluator with the fully-specified deck
4. Return top-N assignments sorted by score

## Output struct
```go
type SlotAssignment struct {
    EvoSlot     *CardWithSlotType  // nil if unused
    ChampionSlot *CardWithSlotType // nil if unused
    FlexSlot    *CardWithSlotType  // nil if unused
    Score       float64
}
```

## Files to create/edit
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_combinator.go
- Wire into cr-api review output: show top assignment in DeckDelta or a new section
- Tests: internal/recommend/slot_combinator_test.go

## Acceptance criteria
- Balloon-evo never appears in evo slot in any output
- Bowler-champion and Bowler-regular-card produce different SlotType values
- Top assignment for ZyLogan surfaces Balloon in champion/flex and Skel-Army in evo slot
close_reason: DefaultSlotScorer added to pkg/recommend, EnumerateAssignments wired into cr-api review output as new Slot Assignment section (human + markdown + JSON). ZyLogan acceptance test added.

title: Add champion ability metadata to deck scoring
description:
Champions are scored solely by rarity weight (1.2x multiplier) but their unique abilities are not modeled, causing systematic mis-scoring:

- Golden Knight: dash resets Inferno Tower/Dragon charge — enormous defensive utility not captured
- Mighty Miner: tunnel ignores ground troops mid-push — offensive utility undervalued
- Skeleton King: soul collection grants self-heal and troop summoning — sustain not modeled
- Archer Queen: invisibility cloak dodges spells — defensive survivability not captured
- Little Prince: guard + ranged — combo potential not modeled
- Monk: reflect ability — spell mitigation not captured

Fix: add a hardcoded champion ability metadata table (pkg/config or pkg/deck/role_classifier.go) keyed by card name. Each entry should define: ability type (reset/tunnel/sustain/evasion), defensive value bonus, offensive value bonus, and any synergy tags (e.g. Golden Knight + Inferno Tower counter = high). Factor ability bonuses into scoreCard() alongside the existing rarity boost.

The CR API exposes no ability stats so this must be static metadata. Values should be tunable constants, not magic numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Golden Knight scores measurably higher in decks where Inferno Tower is a known threat archetype; champion ability bonuses are visible in --verbose output; all existing tests pass
close_reason: Added champion ability metadata table (6 abilities), ChampionAbilityBonus constant (0.08), wired into both scoring paths (scoreCard + scoreCardWithEvolutionInternal), with unit test

title: Auto-detect unlocked evolutions from API card data
description:
The deck builder requires --unlocked-evolutions flag (or UNLOCKED_EVOLUTIONS env var) to apply evolution scoring bonuses, but the CR API already returns evolutionLevel per card in the player's collection. This means evolution bonuses are silently zero unless manually declared.

Fix: when building CardCandidate from analysis data, infer unlocked evolutions from cards where EvolutionLevel > 0 or MaxEvolutionLevel > 0. Populate b.unlockedEvolutions automatically before scoreCard is called.

Key files:
- pkg/deck/builder.go: buildCandidate() at line ~440 — add auto-population of b.unlockedEvolutions from data.EvolutionLevel
- pkg/analysis/card_analyzer.go: lines ~101-125 — EvolutionLevel/MaxEvolutionLevel already flow through UpgradeInfo
- cmd/cr-api/deck_builder_config_runtime.go: configureEvolutions() — flag override should still win over auto-detection

The --unlocked-evolutions flag should remain but become an override rather than the only path. Auto-detection should kick in when the flag is absent and API data has EvolutionLevel > 0.
acceptance_criteria: deck build without --unlocked-evolutions flag correctly scores and labels evolution cards when API data shows EvolutionLevel > 0; flag still overrides auto-detection; existing tests pass
close_reason: Auto-detect unlocked evolutions from API EvolutionLevel > 0 data in BuildDeckFromAnalysis when not explicitly configured via flag or env var

Same enhancement applies to 'deck recommend' when --tag is provided.

title: Add --goal flag to recommendation commands (climb/cheap/cw2/f2p/current-archetype)
description: Every recommendation engine (deck recommend, deck build, analyze, upgrade-impact) currently optimizes a generic score. Real users have explicit goals that should reweight the output: 'climb trophies this season', 'minimum gold to reach competitive', 'Clan War 2 prep (4 decks no repeats)', 'F2P-only path', 'stay on my current archetype'. Add a shared --goal={climb,cheap,cw2,f2p,current-archetype} flag across the recommendation commands. Each goal routes weights differently: e.g. cheap maximizes value/1k-gold, cw2 calls deck war and enforces no card repeats across 4 decks, current-archetype constrains to the player's detected playstyle. Discovered while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV — the tool gave Royal Giant Cycle as best but couldn't tell us whether that fits a 'spend least gold' goal or a 'climb fastest' goal.

title: Deduplicate timestamped CLI JSON artifact save paths
description: Triage finding from 2026-07-08 duplication/refactor pass. cmd/cr-api/upgrade_impact_commands.go, cmd/cr-api/whatif_commands.go, and cmd/cr-api/deck_recommend_mulligan_runtime.go still each hand-roll the same timestamped JSON artifact save flow even after #130 introduced saveTaggedJSONArtifact for player-tagged outputs. All three rebuild the artifact timestamp, join dataDir/subdir, format a base filename plus timestamp suffix, and call storage.WriteJSON directly. Extract a small shared helper for timestamped JSON artifact paths/writes that preserves existing filename shapes (for example scenario_<ts>.json, <deck>_<ts>.json, upgrade_impact_<tag>_<ts>.json) and migrate these callers onto it. Goal is to shrink repeated save logic and keep later artifact naming changes in one place.

title: Deduplicate card-exclusion filtering helpers
description: Triage finding 2026-07-01 duplication/refactor pass. cmd/cr-api/deck_builder_config_runtime.go currently keeps two near-identical exclusion helpers: applyExcludeFilter mutates *deck.CardAnalysis in place for deck build flows, while applyCardExclusions returns a copied deck.CardAnalysis for build-all-strategies. Both rebuild the same trimmed lowercase exclusion map and the same CardLevels filtering loop. Collapse them onto one shared implementation that can support both mutating and copy-returning call sites, then add focused regression coverage for exclude-card behavior so future edits do not reintroduce duplicate paths. Goal is modest code shrink in a command file already carrying structural debt, without changing CLI behavior.
close_reason: Collapsed duplicated exclusion-filter loops into one shared helper and added regression tests.

title: Split archetype command monolith into focused modules
description: Triage finding from 2026-06-19 duplication/refactor pass. cmd/cr-api/archetype_commands.go is still a 727-line mixed-responsibility file that bundles command registration, API/offline loading, CSV/JSON save flows, large tabular formatters, card-role helper functions, player-to-analysis adapters, and adapter types bridging deck/analysis interfaces. Two narrower dedup issues already exist in this area: clash-royale-api-0co (shared artifact save helpers) and clash-royale-api-xh0 (duplicate player-analysis adapters). Even beyond those, the file remains harder to shrink because  and  each carry their own runtime + output + save logic inline, and unrelated helpers like display tier rendering, role finders, number formatting, and strategy/synergy adapters live beside command construction. Split this into focused files such as archetype_commands.go, archetype_runtime.go, archetype_format.go, and archetype_adapters.go, then fold the narrower dedup fixes into the extracted layers. Goal is smaller files, fewer package-private helpers per file, and less drift between archetype subcommands.

title: Decompose legacy main.go command surface
description: Triage finding from 2026-06-19 duplication/refactor pass. cmd/cr-api/main.go is still a 775-line legacy grab bag that mixes root app bootstrap, command registration, player/cards/analyze/playstyle command actions, stdout formatters, CSV export plumbing, and JSON persistence helpers. The file still owns multiple responsibilities that newer command files already split out: addAnalyzeCommand/addPlaystyleCommand wire flags inline; analyzeCommand/playstyleCommand fetch data, run analysis, print output, and conditionally export/save in the same functions; displayPlayerInfo/displayCards/displayAnalysisSummary/displayPlaystyleAnalysis carry large presentation blocks; saveAnalysisData/savePlaystyleData duplicate artifact-save policy now being tracked separately in clash-royale-api-0co. Refactor toward dedicated files for root bootstrap, legacy command builders, runtimes, and output/persistence helpers so command behavior is easier to test and cross-command save/output policy can converge. Keep scope structural: shrink main.go and delete duplicated helper flows rather than changing user-facing behavior.

title: Extract shared CLI artifact save helpers

Multiple command runtimes still hand-roll the same persist-result flow: choose a subdirectory under app data, mkdir it, build a timestamped filename from the player tag or report kind, and call storage.WriteJSON. Concrete duplicates include cmd/cr-api/main.go (saveAnalysisData and savePlaystyleData), cmd/cr-api/deck_budget_runtime.go (saveBudgetResult), and cmd/cr-api/archetype_commands.go (saveArchetypeAnalysis and saveDynamicArchetypeAnalysis). The path naming differs by artifact type, but the control flow and error wrapping are nearly identical. Extract a small command/runtime helper that owns mkdir + timestamped JSON write + tag normalization so these commands only supply artifact kind and payload. This should shrink command files and keep persistence behavior consistent when filenames or directory policy change.
close_reason: Extracted shared tagged JSON artifact save helper and migrated budget/archetype/playstyle writers

title: Consolidate duplicate close-and-log helpers

The call sites split by package rather than semantics: pkg/clashroyale uses closeutil and pkg/leaderboard plus pkg/fuzzstorage use storageutil. This creates duplicate tests/maintenance for a tiny utility and keeps logging behavior inconsistent (package-prefixed vs warning-prefixed). Refactor to one canonical close helper package/API, update all call sites, and delete the duplicate wrapper file. Verify with the existing close/storage package tests plus targeted storage/client tests.

title: Calibrate synergy baseline for archetypes with sparse curated DB coverage
description: After clash-royale-api-yde fixed the 0%-floor synergy bug, archetypes whose recommended decks share no curated synergy pairs (cycle, beatdown, control, midrange for some collections) now score the 25%% baseline while bait/siege/spawndeck/bridge_spam score 60-70%% from real DB hits. With the OverallScore weight of 0.25 on synergy, that creates a ~10-point ranking gap between 'documented' and 'undocumented' archetypes that's driven by curated DB coverage rather than actual deck quality. Two related follow-ups: (a) decide whether 25%% is the right baseline (compare top recommendations against external meta data for a few real player tags), and (b) expand the curated synergy DB (pkg/deck/synergy.go NewSynergyDatabase) to cover cycle/beatdown core pairs so coverage isn't archetype-biased. Reproduce: ./bin/cr-api deck recommend --tag R8QGUQRCV --count 8 --from-analysis.

title: Reconcile project beads docs with CLI schema: 'docs' is not a valid issue type
description:
Hit while filing issues from the #R8QGUQRCV cr-api review on 2026-05-05. The project's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md documents 'docs' as a valid issue type ('Types: task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs' in one section, and a separate 'Issue Types' table that includes 'chore'). The bd CLI rejects --type=docs with 'validation failed for issue : invalid issue type: docs'. Empirically valid types in this workspace appear to be: bug, feature, task, epic, chore, question.

Fix one of two ways:
1. Update CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to drop 'docs' and use 'task' for documentation work (matches the schema and matches what the original retry succeeded with), OR
2. Add 'docs' to the beads CLI's accepted issue types if there's intent to support it.

Option 1 is the cheap correct fix; option 2 only makes sense if the upstream beads tool wants to support it. Repro:
  BD_DOLT_MODE=embedded bd create --title='X' --description='Y' --type=docs --priority=4
  # → Error: validation failed for issue : invalid issue type: docs
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md to reflect the actual valid bd issue types (chore replacing docs); added explicit note that docs is not accepted.

title: Unify combat-stats flags across analyze, upgrade-impact, deck build
description: The same scoring concept is exposed three different ways: 'upgrade-impact' has --use-combat-stats (boolean opt-in), 'deck build' has --combat-stats-weight (0.0-1.0) and --disable-combat-stats, 'analyze' has neither. This is confusing and the inconsistent surface makes scripting harder. Pick one shape (suggest: --combat-stats-weight float with 0 disabling and a sensible default) and adopt it across all three commands.

title: Battle-log scan for non-event ladder games (per-deck win rate over time)
description: 'events scan' covers challenges/tournaments but there is no equivalent for non-event ladder play. Player #R8QGUQRCV has 1236 battles with 55.5% win rate, but we have no way to ask: which deck did they actually use last week, what is their per-deck win rate over time, are they trending up or down. The official API exposes recent battle log; add a scan that aggregates ladder games by detected deck signature and emits per-deck W/L/3-crown stats and a trajectory chart. Feeds the proposed cr-api review orchestrator with real performance data instead of just snapshot scores.

title: Add bulk shard import for evolutions shards (JSON paste / OCR / assume-N mode)
description: 'evolutions recommend' is built around shard counts but the official Clash Royale API doesn't expose shard inventory, and 'evolutions shards set' requires per-card manual entry. The feature is effectively inert today — for player #R8QGUQRCV every card showed 0/10 shards and the recommendations were advisory-only. Need a bulk-import path: (1) JSON paste/file import, (2) optional OCR from a screenshot of the shard inventory screen, (3) a 'assume-N' mode that fills in N shards/card uniformly so recommendations become directional even without exact data. At minimum (1) and (3) are achievable without API changes.

title: Cross-command score normalization or scoring guide appendix
description: Score scales across cr-api commands aren't comparable: 'playstyle' reports 40/100, 'archetypes detect' reports 76.6 (also /100 but a different scale), 'deck evaluate' reports 5.3/10, 'upgrade-impact' reports 377 (unbounded raw points). Nothing in the CLI explains how to read across these. Two options: (a) normalize all user-facing scores to a single 0-100 with documented bands (Optimal 90+, Competitive 75-89, etc), (b) add a 'cr-api help scoring' or short markdown appendix explaining each scale and how they relate. Option (a) is better; (b) is the cheap fallback.

title: Hide or compute 'Key Cards Found' line in upgrade-impact summary
description: 'cr-api upgrade-impact' reports 'Key Cards Found: 0' in the Summary section even when 10 high-impact upgrades are listed below it. The 'key archetype card' bucket appears to be a separate concept that simply isn't populated for this player. Either (a) compute it correctly (define what makes a card 'key' — top archetype win conditions in the player's most viable archetypes?), or (b) hide the line when the bucket is empty so the summary doesn't show misleading zeros. Discovered on player #R8QGUQRCV.
